market_summary: S&P 500 -0.38% to 7,337, Nasdaq -0.13%, Dow -0.63% to 49,597, Russell 2000 -1.74%. Markets pulled back from record highs as Iran peace deal uncertainty lingered — Tehran still reviewing US one-page memorandum to end war and reopen Strait of Hormuz. Energy sector led declines (-1.95%) as oil traded sideways (WTI $95.66, Brent $101.96) on deal expectations. Semiconductors took profits after weeks-long rally (Micron, AMD, Lam Research ~-4%). Planet Fitness -33% after slashing 2026 outlook. HawkEye 360 +28% on NYSE debut. Airbnb revenue +18% YoY to $2.7B, raised 2026 guidance. Paul Tudor Jones said AI bull market has 'another year or two'. Gold $4,685 (-0.13%), Silver ~$80 (+3.89% per Trading Economics but mixed readings across sources). BTC ~$81,023 (-$1,297 from prior day), ETH ~$2,327 (-$81). MSTR at $179.21 (range $175.72-$186.82).
key_events_tomorrow: Fri May 8 08:30 ET: April Nonfarm Payrolls — HIGHEST IMPACT event. Prior: 178K (March, beat consensus 60K). Wage growth expected ~3.7% YoY (prior 3.8%). Strong print could push back rate cut expectations further.
Fri May 8: Senators' deadline for SEC Chair Atkins to respond re: crypto securities law exemptions — could generate headlines.
Ongoing: Iran response to US one-page peace memorandum expected 'within days'. Binary risk for oil, gold, and risk assets.
Earnings after Thu close: Coinbase, Block, Airbnb results already out. Friday pre-market reactions.
May 15: Powell steps down as Fed Chair, Warsh takes over — hawkish pivot risk in 8 days.
overnight_risks: Iran peace deal BINARY RISK: Tehran reviewing US one-page MOU via Pakistan mediators. Deal terms: 12-15yr nuclear moratorium, sanctions lift, frozen funds release, Hormuz reopening. Complication: Iran demands halt to Israel-Hezbollah fighting in Lebanon as precondition. Trump threatened 'much higher level' bombing if no deal. Project Freedom (Hormuz military op) paused but US blockade on Iranian ports remains.

NFP Friday 08:30 ET: March surprised massively to upside (178K vs 60K expected). Another hot print could cement 'no cuts in 2026' narrative. FOMC held 3.5-3.75% on Apr 29 with historic 4-way dissent (first since 1992). Gov. Miran wanted 25bp cut, 3 others wanted hawkish language.

Asia session: South Korea Kospi at records (+70% annual gains, closed 7,385 May 6). Markets broadly constructive on Iran peace hopes. China CSI 300 resuming after Labor Day. Risk: any Iran deal collapse headlines could hit Asia markets hard.
European open: Oil-sensitive. Brent near $100 — if Iran deal materializes, could drop to $80-85. If talks collapse, spike to $130+.
VIX still complacent despite Hormuz closure and active conflict. Equity markets pricing in resolution — asymmetric downside if talks fail.

MSTR: Q1 showed $12.54B loss, Peter Schiff calling it 'Ponzi', STRC dividend sustainability questioned. Analysts split: BTIG raised to $350, Benchmark cut to $570. BTC sales to meet dividends would be structural change from 'never-sell' policy.

instrument_news.XAG-USD: Silver ~$80/oz (mixed readings: Trading Economics $80.32 +3.89%, FXStreet $79.78, Investing.com $80.21). Rose toward $79 extending gains as Iran deal hopes eased oil prices and inflation worries. Gold/silver ratio at 62:1 (lowest since 2013, compressing from historical avg). Silver peaked $121.62 Jan 29 2026 (ATH), now ~34% off peak. Structural deficit: 67M oz annual shortfall, China imports hit ATH March 2026 (retail + solar manufacturers). COT (May 1): net long 24,160 contracts. COMEX backwardation signals physical tightness. Industrial demand intact: solar, EVs, AI data centers.
instrument_news.XAU-USD: Gold $4,685 (-0.13%). Retreated from $4,700+ as Middle East de-escalation hopes reduced safe-haven bid. Higher yields and stronger dollar pressuring. Support near $4,630-4,650. Central bank buying remains structural (~755t projected 2026). JP Morgan target $6,300 by year-end. $82B into gold ETFs YTD. Risk: Iran deal would deflate geopolitical premium 5-8%. Resistance at $4,700-4,720.
instrument_news.BTC-USD: BTC $81,023 at 9:15am ET (-$1,297 from prior day). Pulled back from highest level since January. 9-day ETF inflow streak totaling ~$2.7B, dominated by IBIT ($335M) and FBTC ($185M). May already strongest ETF inflow month of 2026. April closed +$1.97B net positive (best monthly 2026). Tom Lee: closing May above $76K confirms new bull market. Three technical signals pointing to $85K target per CoinDesk. Crypto Fear & Greed recovering from deep fear zone. Market cap $1.33T.
instrument_news.ETH-USD: ETH $2,327 (-$81 from prior day). Trading $2,290-2,350 range, weak relative to BTC. Bears in charge after failing at $2,400 resistance. Market cap ~$233B. Spot ETH ETFs seeing modest inflows but lagging BTC ETF momentum. SEC crypto framework (5 categories, 3 not securities) potentially constructive but implementation unclear. EF selling pressure ($55M recent OTC deals) still weighing on sentiment.
instrument_news.MSTR: MSTR $179.21 (range $175.72-$186.82). Q1 showed $12.54B loss. Peter Schiff labeled it 'Ponzi', warned STRC dividend unsustainable. Analysts divided: BTIG raised target to $350 (Buy), Benchmark cut to $570 (Buy), Canaccord hiked to $224. Key concern: potential shift from 'never-sell' BTC stance to fund dividends. AIMCo (Alberta pension) invested $172M Q1. Company renamed from MicroStrategy to Strategy Inc (Aug 2025).

metals_outlook: Gold consolidating near $4,685 after touching $4,700+. Iran peace deal is the dominant catalyst — deal materializing deflates geopolitical premium 5-8%, failure spikes it. Central bank buying structural (~755t 2026). JP Morgan $6,300 target. Fed on hold 3.5-3.75% with historic 4-dissent split. Silver at ~$80 benefiting from dual tailwinds: (1) safe haven + Iran de-escalation easing inflation fears via oil, and (2) industrial demand structural deficit (67M oz/yr shortfall, China record imports). Gold/silver ratio at 62:1 (lowest since 2013) — analysts expect further compression toward 55-60 if silver industrial thesis plays out. COT positioning still net long. COMEX backwardation. Silver down ~34% from ATH $121.62 (Jan 29) but well-supported by physical tightness. NFP tomorrow could move metals: hot print = stronger dollar = metals pressure; miss = rate cut hopes = metals rally.
oil_outlook: WTI $95.66 (+0.61%), Brent $101.96 (+0.68%). Prices stabilized after sharp declines on Iran deal hopes. Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed — 9.1M bpd shut-in per EIA, 20% of global oil/gas flows disrupted. Key developments: (1) US sent one-page MOU via Pakistan to formally end conflict, Tehran reviewing; (2) Trump paused Project Freedom military op but maintains blockade on Iranian ports; (3) Iran demands Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire as precondition — complicates deal; (4) Trump threatened 'much higher level' bombing if no agreement. OPEC+ raised output 188K bpd (symbolic while Hormuz closed). UAE left OPEC May 1 with ~1M bpd spare capacity (irrelevant until shipping resumes). Binary outcome: deal → oil $80-85, collapse → $130-140. Goldman sees Brent $100+ avg 2026.
crypto_outlook: BTC holding ~$81K after 9-day ETF inflow streak ($2.7B). Tom Lee says $76K close in May confirms bull market. Three technical signals pointing to $85K (CoinDesk). May strongest ETF inflow month of 2026 so far. Regulatory environment constructive: SEC classified 3 of 5 crypto categories as non-securities (Mar 2026), SEC-CFTC MOU signed, Atkins proposing 'innovation exemption'. Senators pressing Atkins on exemptions — response due May 8. ETH lagging ($2,327, failing at $2,400). April ETF net inflows: BTC +$1.97B (best month 2026), recovering from $6.38B outflow period (Nov 2025-Feb 2026). Institutional demand strong: BlackRock IBIT and Fidelity FBTC dominating flows. Risk factors: NFP tomorrow could strengthen dollar and pressure crypto; Iran deal outcome binary for risk assets; MSTR dividend concerns could create BTC selling pressure if Strategy forced to liquidate.
sentiment_shift: Risk-off rotation from records. Equities pulled back from ATH (S&P 7,337, Dow briefly touched 50K) as markets digest Iran uncertainty and await NFP. Semiconductors taking profits after extended rally. Energy sector worst performer (-1.95%) on Iran deal expectations. Crypto retreating modestly but ETF flows remain strongly positive (9-day $2.7B streak). Metals mixed — gold slightly down on de-escalation hopes, silver up on industrial demand. Key inflection: NFP tomorrow is the week's macro event. Hot print cements 'no cuts 2026', cold print revives dovish hopes. Powell-Warsh transition May 15 (8 days away) adds regime uncertainty. Iran deal remains the dominant binary catalyst across all asset classes — resolution timeline 'within days' per Pakistan mediators. VIX still complacent despite Hormuz crisis, suggesting market is heavily positioned for peaceful resolution.
